spec_match.rationale: Simulates source.attribution_scopes.{values,limit} and trigger attribution_scopes arrays with set-intersection semantics, including the source scope_limit truncation that controls the effective source set.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised multi-select source scopes, multi-select trigger scopes, the live numeric scope_limit input, ignored over-limit chips, ARIA pressed state, and the deterministic attribution readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises client-side simulation of the new attribution_scopes field — page delivers it.

edge_cases.rationale: Trigger arrays and limit < scopes.length are now visible. The page still has a minor follow-up for the no-source-scopes legacy branch.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5096560068395008 plus the WICG explainer.

design_system.rationale: All CSS variables, scope-chip override sets both background and color, --accent-amber used on warn block which exists in styles.css.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the empty-scopes branch
openQuestions.detail: A source registered WITHOUT attribution_scopes still matches every trigger (legacy behaviour preserved for backwards compatibility). Add a 'no scopes' chip that clears the source set and shows the trigger always attributes — that's the obvious teaching moment about when to opt in.

summary: Re-reviewed in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp. Before the fix, trigger chips were radio-style and source scopes beyond scope_limit were silently dropped. The simulator now lets trigger attribution_scopes stay multi-selected, reports intersection size and matching scopes, visibly marks selected source scopes over scope_limit as ignored, and lists ignored scopes in the result. DevTools verified matching, non-matching, empty-trigger, and over-limit states with clean console and network logs.
